display.habitableZonesExplanation: Shows the range of orbits around a star within which a planetary surface can support liquid water.
display.barycenterExplanation: The center of mass of two or more bodies that orbit each other.
display.lagrangePointsExplanation: Five points where the gravitational pull of the Sun and Earth combine to let an object orbit in sync with Earth. JWST orbits at L2; Trojan asteroids collect at L4 and L5.
display.labelsExplanation: Show name labels above planets, stars, and moons in the 3D view.
display.trueToScaleExplanation: Renders planets and stars at their true relative sizes. You may need to zoom in to see smaller objects.

graphics.ringPhysicsExplanation: Simulates the gravitational interactions of ring particles. Can be resource intensive.

advanced.perfMonitorExplanation: Displays technical performance data, such as frames per second (FPS).
advanced.showBlackHoleEffectsExplanation: Visualizes the gravitational distortion around black holes.
advanced.highQualityBlackHolesExplanation: Improves the accuracy and visual fidelity of black hole rendering.

support.heading: Support GitUniverse
support.description: If you enjoy exploring the universe, consider supporting GitUniverse's development.
support.bannerMessage: I'm building GitUniverse by myself and want to keep it free and ad-free. If you enjoy exploring the universe, any support is appreciated.
